domain: Tuvan igil 2-string horse-head fiddle (perfect-fifth tuning; sympathetic resonance from bowed-to-unbowed string; implicit overtone voicing from 2-string design)
specific_mechanism: 2-head sympathetic-resonance regularizer that penalises key-subspace alignment off-the-harmonic-series of carrier-head-1; creates implicit ghost-head emergence retrievable at inference without extra parameters.
llm_application: IGIL-DUAL-SYMPATHY: a 2-head attention design where two designated 'carrier' heads in each layer are coupled by a harmonic-series regularizer L_sym = sum_partial ||K_carrier1 * P_partial - K_carrier2||^2 where P_partial projects carrier-1's key subspace onto its harmonic-series partials. The regularizer trains carrier-2 to occupy the harmonic-series subspace of carrier-1, creating an implicit 'ghost head' whose attention pattern is carrier-1's harmonic partial. Inference uses both carriers AND the ghost-head expansion. Differs from MoE-routing (extra params), Shared-QKV (parameter sharing), Linear-Predictability post-hoc analysis, R279 PTCH within-head harmonic by being BETWEEN-head and an EXPLICIT TRAINING OBJECTIVE for ghost-head emergence.
novelty_claim: Linear Predictability of Attention Heads 2603.13314 observes head dependencies post-hoc. Quantifying Head Stability 2602.16740 measures key-subspace alignment as an emergent property. IGIL-DUAL-SYMPATHY explicitly TRAINS for harmonic-series alignment between 2 carriers, creating ghost-head capacity. Small extension of existing post-hoc literature; recombination of harmonic-loss + head-coupling. Expected FAIL with strong neighborhood overlap.
